steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: Harper practiced self-effacement — stepping back, suppressing his own editorial voice to let contributors express diverse perspectives. The blank describes Burke and Lowe, whose editorial decisions diverged from Harper's. The blank must describe the opposite of self-effacement: asserting a strong, fixed editorial vision.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — proficient: Violates word meaning — “proficient” means skilled, but the passage contrasts editorial styles, not skill levels. Nothing implies Harper was less skilled. ✗
  • Choice B — dogmatic: “Dogmatic” means stubbornly committed to one's own beliefs or principles — the direct opposite of Harper's self-effacing approach and consistent with editors who promote a “unified vision” over diverse perspectives. ✓
  • Choice C — ambiguous: Violates word meaning — “ambiguous” means unclear or open to multiple interpretations, but it is Harper who encouraged multiple viewpoints; Burke and Lowe, who diverged from him, would be less ambiguous, not more. ✗
  • Choice D — unpretentious: Violates word meaning — “unpretentious” is close in meaning to self-effacing, which describes Harper, not Burke and Lowe who are his opposites. ✗
That matches Choice B — dogmatic.
